rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VENRICE PALMER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) The sole purpose of this hearing is to determine why there has been no filed response to the Examiner Notes. 2) Examiner Notes: a. The First Supplement filed 02/07/2022 now alleges the assets on hand on March 31, 2021 is a TD Ameritrade Account #4329 with a balance of $344.52. Even though there has been a waiver of account the Examiner must be able to trace the funds from the I&A value of $1,443,843.54 to the assets on hand of $344.52. Please explain what happened to $1,443,499.02 in estate assets. b. On the attorney timesheets please explain why there are the same entries on both the ordinary and extraordinary timesheets. See entries on the extraordinary timesheets for 6/14/2016, 6/15/2016, 06/07/2016, 08/05/2016, and 2 entries on 11/23/2016. It appears the extraordinary request should be reduced by these duplicate entries as they do not appear extraordinary. c. The petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary. [LRSF 14.35 I. 6) a.] d. The proposed order must state specifically what assets are being distributed only alleging distribute the assets of the estate as follows is not sufficient. e. There are no exhibits attached to the filed petition. Please file either a Supplement or Amendment with notice that provides the Exhibits that are described in the petition but not attached.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VIRGINIA RAMOS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/28/2022. Third continuance same issues. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Liabilities should be on a separate schedule in accordance with Probate Code 1063(g) not as part of the assets on hand at the beginning of the account. (See page 2 of Supplement). A "revised account" must be filed as an amendment or amended petition and re-noticed. (CRC 7.3, 7.53). 2) On schedule C - Disbursements please file a receipt for the following entries: 8/16/2019 San Francisco Public Utilities Commission for $7,575.42. The provide documentation does not explain or verify this entry and it appears unusually large. 3) The provided bank statements show an ending balance in account #7636 of $48,445.47 on 11/29 and $48,365.47 on 12/01. Please explain why the account shows the ending balance of $48,911.07 on 11/31/2021. There is no account statement provided for SF Credit Union account. The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (See LRSF 14.34 D.) An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/13/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LAMBERT GENZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/28/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Examiner received unfiled documents which have all been recycled. See LRSF 14.7 C. 1) Petitioner must give 15 day's notice of the petition and file judicial council form DE-121. 2) Page 2 #3.d. must be filled out. 3) Petitioner selected on page 2 #3.e. bond be waived because all heirs at law are adults and have waived bond, however, there are no waivers of bond filed. 4) Please state the relationship to Decedent of each person listed on the attached service list. 5) Please state the name / date of death for Decedent's parents. All heirs must be listed. If the decedent had no known heirs, a declaration to that effect must be filed, setting forth the basis for that conclusion and the efforts made to locate heirs. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. [LRSF 14.18 C.] 6) Petitioner must file Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative (DE-147). 7) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/13/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BOWMAN CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/15/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: If the statutory fee is based on any amount other than the I&A, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] What happened to the other assets on the I&A not reported as having been sold? (Easylink, Southwest, Walt Disney, and 8 others with "no value".) There were also 98 shares of Alphabet, Inc., in the I&A but only 79 shares on hand. There were 410 shares of American Airlines in the I&A but only 76 shares now. Were other assets (Xperi Holding Corp.) purchased? If there was $237,716.77 in cash in Wells Fargo (and $1,116.04 in the Schwab account), how did the executor arrive at the net cash to be distributed in paragraph 16? (There were a greater number of shares of some of the securities but the carry values did not change?) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues (likely, an amendment) must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/6/22. If an amendment is filed, notice of the amendment must be given at least 15 days before the continued hearing date (unless waived by the other trust beneficiaries). Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GERARD O'LOUGHLIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Neither a petition for final distribution nor a report of status with a petition for additional time to administer the estate has been filed yet. Letters were issued on 5/26/21. [Prob. C. Sect. 12200.] Additionally, no I&A has been filed yet. [Prob. C. Sect. 8800(b).] The initial petition for probate did not show the minor's date of birth and never disclosed who the person is who has legal custody of the minor with whom the minor resides. [CRC 7.51(d).] She was reported to be living in the decedend't real property. No guardian ad litem has been appointed to represent the minor's interest. A declaration by the attorney is not a proper report of status and petition for additional time for estate administration, pursuant to Probate Code Section 12200. See also CRC 7.103(c) and Probate Code Section 1023. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 11th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

There was no "supplemental declaration" filed before the 12/20/21 hearing or since then to address the missing beneficiary. The case has not yet been consolidated with the existing case. A notice of related case in insufficient. The earlier case number IS this same trust case. An ex parte petition must be filed to CONSOLIDATE this case with the already existing case, with the earlier case surviving and no further pleadings being permitted in this later case. A copy of the order, once issued, must be filed in both cases. If issues are not addressed at least 10 days before the continued hearing, an appearance may be required on 7/20/22 for the Court to determine if this petiiton should be taken off calendar so that it can be filed in the correct case. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Joint Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement And Termination Of Trust Pursuant To Probate Code Sections 15403 And 15409
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine how to proceed. Matter set for hearing for the court to take a first look at the filings. 2) Whether San Francisco is the proper venue. 3) Notice issues. No GAL appointed for minors Rocco Nikita Petroni, Julietta Luna Irina, and any unborn or unascertained beneficiaries who are the issue of Marco petroni or Peter petroni, only. Jeffrey Nussbaum did not receive notice of the petition. Notice was not given to Mary Ellen Petroni or Lord Leo Petroni individually and instead was only given to their attorneys. 4) Ex Parte petitions filed for GAL for Rocco Nikita Petroni, Any unborn or unascertained beneficiaries who are the issue of Marco Petroni or Peter Petroni, only, and Julietta Luna Irina Petroni filed by Marco Petroni and attorney Daniel Bernhard have not complied with the local rules and haven't been processed. (LRSF 14.11). 5) Petition is not verified by the parties only signed by attorneys. (CRC 7.103). Settlement was executed by the parties. Mary Ellen Petroni is now deceased. 6) The court has received 4 different proposed orders. 3 different orders regarding the joint petition and an order substituting sucessors in interest in place of deceased petitioner.

The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately, conventionally, and confidentially. No Waivers of Bond were attached as Attachment 3e(3) to the petition. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond. [LRSF 14.16 F.] Item 3g(2)(c) should have been answered, as the petitioner is related to the decedent. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/3/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: HIROYUKI KAJIMA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Petition To Use Spouse'S Sperm Post-Mortem
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) There has been no response to the 04/05/2022 tentative ruling. Court to determine whether to take the petition off calendar. a. This tentative ruling is issued by the judicial officer. Petitioner was asked to file a memorandum of points and authorities regarding jurisdiction and venue. The memorandum fails to address convincingly these issues. None of the presented probate provisions authorize a petition of this kind in the probate court and none of the authorities support the requested relief in the context of this petition. If these requests are properly presented to a probate court, Petitioner needs to present them in an estate proceeding for Hiroyuki Kajima after appointment of an estate representative or explain why she need not proceed this way. At the proper time (likely after appointment of an estate representative), Petitioner must file a second amended petition that conforms to requirements of the Probate Code, including proper allegations regarding: (1) statutory basis for petition; (2) jurisdiction; (3) venue; and, (4) factual and legal basis for all relief requested. The second amended petition must contain allegations overcoming the presumption that Hiroyuki Kajima did not intend his gametic material to be used for posthumous conception and making an affirmative showing that Hiroyuki Kajima intended: (1) Petitioner to have "full ownership" over his gametic material after his death (recognizing that gametic material is a unique type of property not governed by general laws regarding gifts or transfers upon death); (2) Petitioner to control the disposition of his gametic material upon his death; and, (3) intended to allow the use of his gametic material for posthumous conception." (See Robertson v. Saadat (2020) 48 Cal.App.5th 630, 640-648.) If Hiroyuki Kajima's intent is not evidence by a writing or writings, the second amended petition should address why a writing should not be required, as it is in the heirship context under Probate Code section 249.5. (See id. at p. 647 [reserving decision whether intent must be evidenced by writing].) Amended petition must include a copy of the sperm storage agreement, as well as copies of the executed UCSF forms referenced in the amended petition. The second amended petition must be properly noticed and the papers properly served, with proofs of notice and service to all parties entitled to notice of an estate proceeding and all parties against whom relief is sought.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STANLEY MARLAND COFFIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/14/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The clerks have advised that no original will has been lodged. [Probate Code Section 8200(a)(1).] The petition fails to identify the trustee of the devisee trust and all trust beneficiaries, if the trustee is the petitioner, must be identified in item 8 and must be given notice. The copy of the trust attached to the petition indicates that there is also a trust protector. He must be listed in item 8 of the petition and must be given notice. Was there a personal property memorandum? The verification of the supplement filed on 5/12/22 refers to the supplement filed on 5/23/22..? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues (e.g., a second supplement) must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/3/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/21/2022 by the assigned judicial officer after review. 1) A new citation must be issued and timely served on the respondent spouse alleged to lack capacity. 2) The Court will appoint an investigator. 3) The Court will appoint legal counsel for the respondent spouse. 4) An additional explanation must be provided as to why the respondent spouse's interest in the assets must be transmuted to the petitioner spouse as his sole and separate property at this time and why removing the respondent spouse's name from the assets should not suffice.
